question: Can direct rocWMMA accumulator-fragment access remove enough producer overhead to materially reduce the sparse dKV many-to-one accumulation wall?

tile_gate.read: Direct c_frag[] access compiles and gives an order-insensitive checksum matching store_matrix_sync/reference. This proves direct fragment access can feed scalar epilogues, but rocWMMA documents fragment element order as not geometrically stable, so this alone does not provide a row/group mapping for route-aware dKV reduction.

s4096_route_grid_gate.read: The accumulator LDS spill is not a meaningful part of the S4096 producer wall in this rocWMMA route-grid schedule. Removing store_matrix_sync from the timing sink saves only about 0.07 ms, while the no-dKV-write contribution sink remains about 9.55 ms.

decision.reason: Direct fragment access is valid as a diagnostic, but it does not materially reduce S4096 producer latency and does not solve row/group-aware segmented reduction. The bottleneck is not the accumulator spill; it is the contribution-production schedule plus route-aware write/reduction behavior.
decision.next_branch: Do not spend more time on store_matrix_sync removal in rocWMMA. The next viable branch is a lower-level MFMA/CK-Tile/TileLang producer that preserves query-major reuse and performs lightweight segmented/grouped reduction before global writes, or a fused sparse-MLA backward boundary that avoids diagnostic P/dP/materialized partial traffic entirely.
